text:
Published

Jul 09, 2024 at 05:35 AM EDT

News Reporter

Vacationers planning to travel by train between Belgium, France, and the U.K. in the second half of August are set to face delays.

The Eurostar, a high-speed rail line between Belgium and the French border will be shut down for engineering works, extending journey times by about half an hour.

This closure, announced by Belgium's train operator SNCB, rail network infrastructure operator Infrabel, and Eurostar, aims to address signs of aging in the rail infrastructure.

Infrabel has identified aging issues in thehigh-speedline connecting Brussels to the French-Belgian border.

This section, the oldest on Belgium's high-speed network, is now 27 years old. To tackle these issues, Infrabel has planned a decade-long renewal project.

While most of the work will be done at night, a two-week full closure of the line each summer will be necessary.

The engineering works will begin on August 12, immediately following the closing ceremony of the ParisOlympics, which will take place on August 11.

Over 18 days and nights, technical teams will overhaul one of the two tracks on a 17.6 kilometer stretch between the Belgian towns of Leuze-en-Hainaut and Brugelette. This involves laying 35.2 km of new rails, 30,000 new sleepers, and 5,500 tons of ballast.

During this period, Eurostar trains between Brussels and destinations such as Lille, Amsterdam, and Cologne will be diverted onto classic lines, adding approximately 30 minutes to travel times.

SNCB trains will also be adjusted, affecting national journeys between Brussels, Mons, Ath, and Mouscron.

Additionally, from August 12 to October 25, Austrian rail operator ÖBB will suspend its Nightjet service between Brussels and Berlin due to ongoing works in France and Germany. The European Sleeper night trains will continue to operate between Brussels and Berlin, extending to Prague via Amsterdam.

Travelers should prepare for longer journey times and potential schedule changes during the high-speed rail closure.

SNCB, Infrabel, and Eurostar are coordinating to minimize disruption, but passengers are advised to check updated schedules and plan accordingly.

As the renewal project continues over the next decade, annual summer closures can be expected, each involving extensive maintenance and upgrades.

The current phase is crucial to ensuring the long-term safety and reliability of thehigh-speed railnetwork, with future works likely to follow a similar pattern.

In the interim, passengers traveling between Belgium, France, and the U.K. should anticipate delays and consider alternative routes or travel methods if necessary.

title: High-Speed Rail Closure Will Delay Vacationers - Newsweek
description: The Eurostar which runs between Belgium and the French border will be shut for engineering works, extending journey times by about half an hour.
